current_decision: Maintain direct PostgreSQL transaction + fail-closed/write suspend now; treat PostgreSQL HA and durable queue-first architecture as follow-up availability and V2 contract candidates.

decision_matrix.note: Scores are deterministic project-fit signals, not production benchmarks.
Scores are deterministic project-fit signals, not production benchmarks.

follow_up_candidates: managed DB HA runbook and failover drill
consumer idempotency and DLQ replay design
RPO/RTO split for API accept and ledger posting
manual_approval_required: write resume after failover or restore
non_scope: Kafka, RabbitMQ, SQS, or cloud resource provisioning

options.api_response_semantics: COMPLETED only after PostgreSQL commit; 503 + Retry-After when write path is unavailable.
options.name: Current: Direct PostgreSQL Transaction + Fail-Closed
options.risk_summary: Lower write availability during DB outage, but no ambiguous success response.
options.write_durability_model: PostgreSQL commit is the durability boundary.

options.api_response_semantics: COMPLETED remains primary commit based; failover windows return 503 until consistency gate and write resume approval complete.
options.name: PostgreSQL Primary/Standby HA
options.risk_summary: Stale connections, split brain risk, and replication lag during failover.

options.api_response_semantics: COMPLETED still means commit, but commit latency and standby availability become part of the write path.
options.name: Synchronous Replication
required_new_controls: write suspend on standby quorum loss
options.risk_summary: Lower RPO, higher commit latency, and possible write stalls.
options.write_durability_model: Commit waits for synchronous standby or quorum acknowledgement.

options.api_response_semantics: COMPLETED remains database commit based; application still handles connection retry, readiness, consistency gate, and write resume approval.
options.name: Managed DB HA
options.risk_summary: Cloud dependency, cost, and application-level failover handling remain.
options.write_durability_model: Managed primary/standby or multi-AZ durability boundary.

options.api_response_semantics: API returns ACCEPTED for durable enqueue; COMPLETED is emitted only after consumer idempotency, PostgreSQL commit, and reconciliation evidence.
options.name: Durable Queue-First Architecture
required_new_controls: ACCEPTED vs COMPLETED API contract split
options.risk_summary: Accept/posting split, DLQ, replay, offset, and reconciliation complexity.
options.write_durability_model: Queue durability first, PostgreSQL ledger posting later.

phase: PH8 PostgreSQL HA / Durable Queue Trade-off ADR

recommended_later: Managed DB HA for production availability after failover drills
Queue-first V2 only after ACCEPTED/COMPLETED API contract split
Consumer idempotency, DLQ, replay, offset evidence, and reconciliation
recommended_now: Fail-closed 503 + Retry-After when PostgreSQL write path is unavailable
Write suspend, recovery case, and consistency gate before write resume
